message: Web UI tweaks: real-link results, URL-persisted query + localStorage recent-search dropdown, markdown-it message rendering, user/assistant coloured boxes, opencode in tagline, leaner result rows (id/filename only on session page), sticky session header.

Capture per-session working directory from log content (adapter.extractCwd for claude/codex/pi; opencode session.directory) into source_files.cwd; resolve per session via chunks join; one-time backfillCwd at startup for pre-existing data; session API returns it home-relative. Filename-decoding was rejected: lossy (slashes->dashes, ambiguous for dashed dirs like agent-search) and absent for codex/opencode. Title bar shows session id + copy and working dir + copy-log-path.

Copy buttons fall back to hidden-textarea execCommand('copy') when navigator.clipboard is absent, so copy works on insecure http:// origins (not just https/localhost). New web/src/lib/clipboard.ts copyText helper.

web: replace separate search/session pages with a persistent resizable search sidebar + main session panel; removed transcript line numbers and the header back button; q carried in URL across session nav

Search cwd scoping uses exact cwd match, not prefix: confirmed consistent with claude's own behaviour (resuming from a subdirectory doesn't surface sibling-directory sessions in the same repo). --all opts out.
sift show: added line-range support (id:LINE, id:A-B, --lines/-l RANGE) and per-subcommand --help (sift show --help etc.)

claude adapter: key sidechain (subagent) records by filename stem, not the embedded parent sessionId — subagent transcripts were folding into the parent session and appending after its last message in the web view

codex adapter: normalize message/function_call_output content (string OR typed-block array) to text via blocksToText — view_image outputs are image-block arrays, which left chunk.text as an object and broke the SQLite insert

indexFile: commit chunk insert + tail-state advance in one transaction (parse first, outside it) so a parse/insert error rolls back the offset and the next pass re-reads — previously the offset advanced first and errors silently dropped a file's content
Repo is public: web dev server defaults to localhost, LAN exposure opt-in via VITE_HOST/VITE_ALLOWED_HOSTS. No personal hostnames in committed config. Added top-level README.
